1945:51418.name: Button
1945:51418.description:
ukg-button

Buttons are one-tap actions. They feature a range of weights to communicate their emphasis. They are configurable with parent and child icon options. Buttons can also have their primary icon, label, and secondary icon turned off to create different configurations.

2369:44542.name: Type - Data
2369:44542.description: Use these styles for short snippets of data. Data is more compact compared to Copy which makes it good for saving horizontal space.

540:2705.name: Avatar
540:2705.description:
.ukg-avatar

Avatars provide a consistent visual representation of a specific person.

721:16298.name: Divider
721:16298.description:
.ukg-divider

Dividers separate content areas from each other.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Status colors help convey extra meaning to icons and contextual messages. These colors are not themable, so they will not change in white-labeling situations.

children.name: Text
children.text: Status colors help convey extra meaning to icons and contextual messages. These colors are not themable, so they will not change in white-labeling situations.

componentProperties.name: Content#3573:1006
componentProperties.value: In all cases, shape or other context must accompany these colors to help convey their meaning to people with color vision deficiencies. Do not rely on color alone as a means of communicating status.

children.name: Text
children.text: In all cases, shape or other context must accompany these colors to help convey their meaning to people with color vision deficiencies. Do not rely on color alone as a means of communicating status.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Avatars provide a consistent visual representation of a specific person. The colors displayed are used for text avatars which can be used on dark or light surfaces.

children.name: Text
children.text: Avatars provide a consistent visual representation of a specific person. The colors displayed are used for text avatars which can be used on dark or light surfaces.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Badges highlight important pieces of text. Badges either contain a value or an icon. The colors are only used for badges and should not be used for data visualization colors.

children.name: Text
children.text: Badges highlight important pieces of text. Badges either contain a value or an icon. The colors are only used for badges and should not be used for data visualization colors.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Use low-emphasis borders in situations where a mid-emphasis border is too loud. Use cases like repeated dividers in a table or a list are ideal.

children.name: Text
children.text: Use low-emphasis borders in situations where a mid-emphasis border is too loud. Use cases like repeated dividers in a table or a list are ideal.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: When an actionable container using low-emphasis borders is hovered, the borders will increase in contrast.

children.name: Text
children.text: When an actionable container using low-emphasis borders is hovered, the borders will increase in contrast.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Use mid-emphasis borders as the default color for all generic borders and dividers that do not have special considerations.

children.name: Text
children.text: Use mid-emphasis borders as the default color for all generic borders and dividers that do not have special considerations.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Use the high-emphasis border in cases where a 3:1 contrast ratio boundary is required. This is necessary for elements like form field edges or other boundaries that need to meet WCAG 2.1 AA visbility requirements.

children.name: Text
children.text: Use the high-emphasis border in cases where a 3:1 contrast ratio boundary is required. This is necessary for elements like form field edges or other boundaries that need to meet WCAG 2.1 AA visbility requirements.

componentProperties.name: Text#2370:0
componentProperties.value:
#FFFFFF @ 43% opacity
3.07:1 on surface_dark
3.71:1 on surface_darkDarker
3:1 on brand

children.name: Text
children.text:
#FFFFFF @ 43% opacity
3.07:1 on surface_dark
3.71:1 on surface_darkDarker
3:1 on brand

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Button toggle requires unique background tokens to create enough contrast

children.name: Text
children.text: Button toggle requires unique background tokens to create enough contrast

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Chips require unique tokens for their tinted idle and hover states. This was done to make them stand out a bit more from the standard surface-light-darker background color.

children.name: Text
children.text: Chips require unique tokens for their tinted idle and hover states. This was done to make them stand out a bit more from the standard surface-light-darker background color.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value:
Use these colors as a family when colors need to be differentiated.  Always use them in combination with different shapes to ensure the best result for people with CVD. See the badges component for a useage example.

Please consult with the DLS team before implementing to ensure WCAG compliance.

For more information on this palette see: https://jfly.uni-koeln.de/color/

For more information on this palette see: https://jfly.uni-koeln.de/color/

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: The highlight range tints are used for highlighting selections a person has made. They are used for highlighting selected rows in the data table, and selecting date ranges.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: The scrim tint is used behind any modal foreground element such as a dialog or custom modal. It darkens content in the background so people know the background content is not currently interactive.

children.name: Text
children.text: The scrim tint is used behind any modal foreground element such as a dialog or custom modal. It darkens content in the background so people know the background content is not currently interactive.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Color supports our brand and reinforce our design language. Colors are designated for specific purposes. Apply colors by selecting a shape and choosing them from the Appearance > Layer Style menu.

children.name: Text
children.text: Color supports our brand and reinforce our design language. Colors are designated for specific purposes. Apply colors by selecting a shape and choosing them from the Appearance > Layer Style menu.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: The light surfaces are the common backgrounds for page content. All "on-light" text and icon colors are designed to be contrast compliant with these shades.

children.name: Text
children.text: The light surfaces are the common backgrounds for page content. All "on-light" text and icon colors are designed to be contrast compliant with these shades.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: The dark surface is used for our left navigation background and some componentry that requires a high-contrast like the snackbar.  All "on-dark" text and icon colors are designed to be contrast compliant with these shades.

children.name: Text
children.text: The dark surface is used for our left navigation background and some componentry that requires a high-contrast like the snackbar.  All "on-dark" text and icon colors are designed to be contrast compliant with these shades.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: The brand color is a theme and branding color. It is currently used for the top Navigation header. It is expected that this color will be replaced by a custom color set for white-label theming.

children.name: Text
children.text: The brand color is a theme and branding color. It is currently used for the top Navigation header. It is expected that this color will be replaced by a custom color set for white-label theming.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Used to highlight prominent actions. This color will appear on buttons, links and other actionable controls. This color is not customer themable as accessibility on actions is critical. Blue is used as it can be seen by the largest number of people.

children.name: Text
children.text: Used to highlight prominent actions. This color will appear on buttons, links and other actionable controls. This color is not customer themable as accessibility on actions is critical. Blue is used as it can be seen by the largest number of people.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value:
Action text colors are used to ensure contrast compliance on hyperlink and button use cases, including when used on top of highlight-range and highlight-range hover backgrounds.

The darker $mtr-action-text color also helps thin action text to visually match the $mtr-action color used on thicker action-colored icons, such as in the buttons.

children.name: Text
children.text:
Action text colors are used to ensure contrast compliance on hyperlink and button use cases, including when used on top of highlight-range and highlight-range hover backgrounds.

The darker $mtr-action-text color also helps thin action text to visually match the $mtr-action color used on thicker action-colored icons, such as in the buttons.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Tokens are wrappers that store the design language atoms that make up Ignite. By changing token values without changing their names, we can effect meaningful changes across Ignite’s base building blocks.

children.name: Text
children.text: Tokens are wrappers that store the design language atoms that make up Ignite. By changing token values without changing their names, we can effect meaningful changes across Ignite’s base building blocks.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value:
1.	Use clear, human readable token names that are easy to understand  
	2.	Do not use homonyms, as they lead to a lack of clarity. (Ex: “type” means both typography and a type of thing) 
	3.	If a token is likely to change in the future, abstract its name from its values so the values can be changed without having to change the name 
	4.	Use names that allow for adding levels between tokens where it is likely to be needed

children.name: Text
children.text:
1.	Use clear, human readable token names that are easy to understand  
	2.	Do not use homonyms, as they lead to a lack of clarity. (Ex: “type” means both typography and a type of thing) 
	3.	If a token is likely to change in the future, abstract its name from its values so the values can be changed without having to change the name 
	4.	Use names that allow for adding levels between tokens where it is likely to be needed

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Reference tokens are base building block tokens that contain a single value such as a color, size, or font property. They allow for governance of allowed attributes and quick tweaking of base values. These tokens are always abstracted from any kind of specific use case.

children.name: Text
children.text: Reference tokens are base building block tokens that contain a single value such as a color, size, or font property. They allow for governance of allowed attributes and quick tweaking of base values. These tokens are always abstracted from any kind of specific use case.

componentProperties.name: Text#2301:0
componentProperties.value: 2. System tokens, for expressions of design language

children.name: Text
children.text: 2. System tokens, for expressions of design language

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: System tokens contain meaningful expressions of design language for all styles and components in the system.  When naming system tokens, focus their names on their usage. This allows value changes to be made to serve a direct purpose.

children.name: Text
children.text: System tokens contain meaningful expressions of design language for all styles and components in the system.  When naming system tokens, focus their names on their usage. This allows value changes to be made to serve a direct purpose.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Complex system tokens can be built with both reference tokens and other system tokens. In this example, a heading token is being built with reference tokens for family, weight and size, and a system token for color

children.name: Text
children.text: Complex system tokens can be built with both reference tokens and other system tokens. In this example, a heading token is being built with reference tokens for family, weight and size, and a system token for color

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value: Use system tokens for theming. As system tokens express the design language, this will give the most predictable results when token values are changed.

children.name: Text
children.text: Use system tokens for theming. As system tokens express the design language, this will give the most predictable results when token values are changed.

componentProperties.name: Text#2362:4
componentProperties.value:
Token strings have an order that their defining characteristics are placed in. This order ensures that tokens are easily understood and comparable. Both reference tokens and system tokens use the same schema.

camelCase and underscores are used to ensure all token names can be used as variables in CSS, LESS, SASS and JS.

children.name: Text
children.text:
Token strings have an order that their defining characteristics are placed in. This order ensures that tokens are easily understood and comparable. Both reference tokens and system tokens use the same schema.

camelCase and underscores are used to ensure all token names can be used as variables in CSS, LESS, SASS and JS.
